root_text.sn55.18:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—jetavane antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
root_text.sn55.18:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā tāvatiṁsakāyikā devatāyo yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā devatāyo āyasmā mahāmoggallāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.18:2.1: “Sādhu kho, āvuso, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn55.18:2.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.18:2.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, āvuso, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn55.18:2.6: sādhu kho, āvuso, ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.18:2.7: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, āvuso, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.sn55.18:3.1: “Sādhu kho, mārisa moggallāna, buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn55.18:3.2: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.18:3.3: Buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.sn55.18:3.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanaṁ hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.18:3.7: Ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgamanahetu kho, mārisa moggallāna, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.18:1.2: And then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared among the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn55.18:1.3: Then several deities of the company of the thirty-three went up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, and stood to one side. Moggallāna said to them:
translation_text.sn55.18:2.1: “Reverends, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha. …
translation_text.sn55.18:2.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn55.18:2.4: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching. …
translation_text.sn55.18:2.6: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.18:2.7: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn55.18:3.1: “My good fellow Moggallāna, it’s good to have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.18:3.3: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn55.18:3.4: It’s good to have experiential confidence in the teaching. …
translation_text.sn55.18:3.6: and to have the ethical conduct that’s loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.18:3.7: It’s the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
